metrics.narrative: BMI's ROI case was built on the 1 Dec 2025 discovery call: a Zycus solution consultant walked Venkata Masetty through a complimentary ROI questionnaire and shared benchmark ranges of 0.6-1.5% incremental sourcing savings on addressable spend and 30-40% FTE efficiency gains, with a preliminary estimate near EUR 2.4M in sourcing savings. Venkata Masetty actively scrutinized the math, asking 'How does this amount of sourcing savings to 2,400,000 or 4,000,000, 6,000,000, these numbers?' and 'Can I expect a 30% efficiency gain, or would it stay something between 20%?' (1 Dec 2025). That is real engagement with the business case, but not yet a buyer-owned, signed-off number, and no update has been logged since.

champion.narrative: Venkata Masetty has driven this deal essentially single-handedly from the 1 Dec 2025 discovery call through RFP submission (8 May 2026) to organizing and leading the 17 Jun 2026 demo with six additional BMI colleagues in the room -- real, sustained internal ownership. What remains unproven is his access to power: he referenced preparing a business case 'for management' since December 2025 with no confirmed follow-through, and no evidence shows him having secured executive sponsorship or budget sign-off. CRM MEDDPICC agrees he is 'the strongest active contact' but there is 'not yet enough evidence to call him a fully proven internal champion.'

competition.narrative: No named competitor has surfaced in any call or field to date. The only vendor name mentioned across both deep-read calls is Coupa, and only as context: 'Coupa mentioned as Caroline's prior employer (context only); no competitor vendors evaluated by BMI stated in meeting' (17 Jun 2026 demo notes). Given BMI ran a formal RFP process (submitted 8 May 2026) for what CRM MEDDPICC describes as a broad 'supplier integrated platform' search, it is plausible other vendors are or were in the mix, but none has been confirmed in any Zycus-side conversation.

identify_pain.narrative: BMI's pain is well-articulated and consistent across both deep-read calls. On 17 Jun 2026, BMI described roughly 40,000 supplier records spread across SAP and non-SAP ERPs, no standardized onboarding (mixing procurement-initiated forms with public self-registration links), duplicate supplier records, and a need to centralize supplier risk/ESG/performance data with SCAR tracking. On 1 Dec 2025 the team had already flagged the need for 'a more efficient onboarding process for suppliers that allows them to fill in their information directly through a provided link.'

paper_process.narrative: No contracting artifact -- MSA, SOW, Order Form -- has been mentioned in any call or task to date. The deal is still inside the RFP-response / demo phase (RFP submitted 8 May 2026, demo 17 Jun 2026), which is normal for Formal Evaluation; the paper process genuinely has not started.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has appeared on any of the 4 discovered calls in the roughly 11 months since this opportunity was created (13 Aug 2025). CRM MEDDPICC (last updated 20 Mar 2026) states the economic buyer 'is not yet clearly identified,' and Venkata Masetty told the team on 1 Dec 2025 he was 'preparing an internal business case to present to management for budget approval' -- meaning the actual approver still sits outside every conversation Zycus has had. Sharon Moar (Group Category Director) and Jeremy Dussel (Procurement Director) joined the 17 Jun 2026 demo, raising the seniority in the room, but neither has been confirmed as the financial approver, and no CFO/CPO-level sponsor has surfaced.

decision_process.narrative: The evaluation mechanics are visible and moving: RFP submitted 8 May 2026, a Zycus RFP Q&A on 6 May 2026, a follow-up RFP call on 28 May 2026 covering commercials and the supplier network, commercials shared 3 Jun 2026 (Core Modules, and Core + Optional Modules), and the deep-dive demo on 17 Jun 2026. What is not visible is the internal sign-off chain behind it: CRM MEDDPICC 2.0 (20 Mar 2026) records the decision process as unclear, and an unnamed executive ('EO') was referenced as expected to decide around early May 2026 with no confirmation since. No one has named who signs off once Venkata Masetty's evaluation is complete.

decision_criteria.narrative: BMI's RFI/RFP made the evaluation criteria explicit: a proven off-the-shelf SaaS platform with a single interface spanning supplier vetting/onboarding, collaboration, performance, risk/compliance, contract management, reporting and e-sourcing, scalable and secure, and able to integrate with ERPs and third-party risk-data providers. The 17 Jun 2026 demo walked through each of these areas module-by-module (iSupplier onboarding/duplicate-check, iRisk/Merlin Risk Radar, iContract InstaReview and e-sign, integrations via iSaaS and the S/4HANA connector) directly against that stated criteria set.

The only named vendor, Coupa, was clarified on the 17 Jun 2026 demo as Caroline Lacocque's prior employer mentioned in passing, not a rival BMI is evaluating; that reference is retired below. Because BMI ran a formal RFI/RFP process, other vendors are likely in the mix, but none have surfaced by name on any call or field to date -- the real competitive exposure is invisibility, not a defined threat. Zycus's positioning strength is its single organically-built suite plus AI/Merlin roadmap, which maps closely to BMI's stated RFI requirements.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A Formal Evaluation deal with genuine, recent buyer engagement (RFP submitted, commercials shared, a well-received deep demo) but no economic buyer mapped after 11 months, which keeps win confidence in check even as near-term momentum looks reasonably healthy.

deal_risk.text: With the close date at 31 Jul 2026 (22 days from today), vendor selection, EB sign-off and contracting have not begun.
BMI's AI-data-governance concern raised 17 Jun 2026 has only a verbal answer so far, with written contractual assurance still outstanding -- a potential blocker heading into contracting.

win_position.text: BMI submitted a formal RFP on 8 May 2026 and sat through a full module-by-module demo on 17 Jun 2026 with six stakeholders in the room, well past a casual-interest stage.
Pain and decision criteria are both clearly and consistently articulated (fragmented supplier data across ~40,000 records, need for a unified SaaS platform), reducing qualification ambiguity.

No economic buyer or executive sponsor has joined a single call in the roughly 11 months since this opportunity opened, and Venkata Masetty's promised 'management' business case, referenced 1 Dec 2025, has never been confirmed as delivered.
No competitor has been named anywhere in the record, leaving the competitive picture unverified rather than confirmed favorable.

deal_momentum.text: Two-way engagement is recent and real: Sharon Moar requested a follow-up session after the 17 Jun 2026 demo, and Caroline Lacocque sent answers to BMI's questions on 25 Jun 2026.
Commercials (two pricing tiers) were shared 3 Jun 2026, moving the conversation into money territory.
No dated future milestone or buyer-committed decision date is on record; a referenced 'EO' decision timeline from around early May 2026 was never confirmed.

customer_commitment.text: Venkata Masetty has personally driven the process for 11 months: discovery, business case prep, RFP submission, and organizing a six-person internal demo.
Beyond Venkata Masetty, engagement from Sharon Moar, Jeremy Dussel, Julia Alenina and Estela Barbero has been limited to attending the 17 Jun 2026 demo; none has independently followed up since.

hard.ais_why:
• Dec 2025 Avoma notes from a meeting with BMI's Venkat capture an explicit Zycus action item to 'provide additional materials on Generative AI in procurement and use-case examples from existing clients', plus discussion of Merlin and Teams-based intake. The customer-side ask for GenAI procurement materials and use-case examples is a buyer-originated AI signal, not Zycus rep-led positioning. Merlin and Merlin Intake are in deal scope alongside iSource, iRisk and iContract.
• Formal Evaluation stage with BD Engaged. Venkat is preparing an internal business case for year-end with budget approval pending; commercial discussion captured pricing of USD $220K/yr + $380-400K implementation. ROI workshop and RFP template have been requested by the customer. Last activity May 2026. The deal momentum is anchored in BMI's internal budget-approval cycle ahead of formal RFP.
• High confidence: SF Description was null but Avoma fills the gap decisively with GenAI follow-up requests and an engaged economic buyer (Venkat) preparing a business case. Held at 7 (not 8+) because customer's primary questions are still ROI/efficiency-focused rather than an explicit AI mandate or POC. A buyer-stated 'AI as selection criterion' would push to 8-9.
